title: Count Landmasses in a Grid
description:
Given an m×n grid of '1' (land) and '0' (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.

Constraints:
- 1 ≤ m, n ≤ 300
explanation:
Intuition
Each unvisited '1' starts a flood fill that marks the entire island.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Scan all cells
2. Main Algorithm: On '1' not yet visited, BFS/DFS to mark all touches
3. Return Result: Increase island count for each new flood

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(mn)
- Space Complexity: O(mn) in worst-case recursion/queue

Key Insights
- Mutate grid or track visited to avoid repeats
- Use 4-directional adjacency

Common Pitfalls
- Diagonal neighbors do not connect
- Forgetting to mark visited when enqueuing can duplicate work

testCases.explanation: When num_islands(grid=["0"]), the expected result is 0
